study_area: San Francisco Bay and Port of Oakland Approaches
disclaimer: Historical AIS first-pass screening evidence demo. Not real-time, not for navigation, not for enforcement, and not for operational decision-making.

predicted_to_observed_abs_delta_min.eligibility: primary observable and common coverage within predicted closest time +/- tolerance

workload_compression.key_message: The pipeline aggregates 1,114,862 cleaned AIS observations into 57 spatial review units containing 5,389 candidate episodes; actual analyst review time was not measured.

stages.interpretation: Behavioral candidates selected for review, not event labels.
Pair records generated from future CPA/TCPA screening.
Repeated time-bin records merged into pair-day audit episodes.
Episodes with synchronized future AIS separation within the screening threshold.
Spatial triage layer for human review; cells are not incident labels.
Small explanation set for audit and presentation.

ranking_evaluation.interpretation: internal dual evidence-target review prioritization; not out-of-sample operational utility, safety prediction, or near-miss accuracy

full_window.interpretation: dual-target review prioritization; not accident or near-miss accuracy

leave_one_day_out.evaluation_design: For each held-out day, traffic-pattern cells and dominant directions are learned only from the other dates; the frozen fold model then rescored both training and held-out feature files.
leave_one_day_out.held_out_isolation: Held-out rows do not contribute to pattern thresholds, regular cells, dominant directions, or training behavior scores.

behavior_refit_provenance.evaluation_design: fold-specific six-day traffic-pattern refit and scoring

ablation.interpretation: High-density cells include berth/anchorage behavior and are not sufficient as safety evidence.
Finds behavior-evidence deviations but misses dense crossing/meeting evidence.
Captures close-quarters candidates but lacks behavioral context.
Combines behavior evidence and future encounter-candidate evidence.

geometric_controls.interpretation: Future geometric support comparison for candidate screening; not prediction accuracy, an accident or near-miss label, or an enforcement/navigation conclusion.

matching.normalized_nearest_objective: minimum sum of six normalized absolute differences, then the six-term component tuple and stable control anchor id
matching.missing_value_rule: all six normalized-objective values must be finite on both sides

matching_balance.definition: SMD=(candidate mean-control mean)/pooled sample SD; DCPA and TCPA are excluded because they define candidate assignment.

within_0_5_nm.denominator_definition: observable geometric-outcome anchors in the selected candidate/control anchor sample after candidate-window exclusion and control thinning

within_1_0_nm.denominator_definition: observable geometric-outcome anchors in the selected candidate/control anchor sample after candidate-window exclusion and control thinning

both_prediction_current_distance_above_0.condition: candidate and matched control current distance at t0 both exceed 0.5 nm

both_prediction_current_distance_above_1.condition: candidate and matched control current distance at t0 both exceed 1 nm

schema_migration.reason: The evaluated denominator is the selected candidate/control anchor sample after candidate-window exclusion and control thinning, not all pair opportunities and not a candidate-independent outcome cohort.

geometric_controls.uncertainty_boundary: Connected-component bootstrap preserves repeated candidate or control vessel-pair/day use within the bipartite match graph. Dependence between separate components is assumed negligible.

cohort_selection.local_exposure_definition: complete opportunity-record count in the date/analysis cell; within-day tied-midrank tertile; missing cell ids fall back to a 0.005-degree cell
